title: Montenegro-Calla's Medium: an optimized, low-cost culture formulation for axenic growth of Naegleria fowleri
abstract: An optimized, serum-free, low-cost axenic medium for Naegleria fowleri that reduces cost by over 70 percent versus commercial alternatives while achieving roughly three times the cell yield, with reproducible passage timing across replicates.
contributions: Roughly three times higher cell yield, reaching 1.03 x 10^7 cells/mL at 96 hours
Over 70 percent cost reduction versus commercial alternatives, with documented sourcing for each component
Reproducible protocol with quality-control checkpoints and a stable passage cadence

links.status: Under review at Journal of Eukaryotic Microbiology; currently in revision

title: Targeting ER stress and mitochondrial pathways in Naegleria fowleri
abstract: Tunicamycin and thapsigargin-induced endoplasmic reticulum stress and metformin-driven mitochondrial dysfunction as therapeutic pathways against Naegleria fowleri, the near-uniformly fatal amoeba behind primary amoebic meningoencephalitis.
contributions: ER stress induction assessed by Western blot for BiP/GRP78
Mitochondrial dysfunction assessed by JC-1 staining and fluorometric assays
